answer: On September 15, 2026, the settlement_disbursement_recon_v3 pipeline deployed a bad join key (normalized_tax_id) when joining escrow_disbursement_fact to party_identity_bridge, causing duplicate matches on buyer and seller party roles within the same closing. This fan-out over-stated escrow disbursement reconciliation variance for Florida purchase closings under FNF National Title in the finance.disbursement_recon_daily view. The fix was to revert the join to closing_party_role_id, quarantine normalized_tax_id joins in reconciliation workloads, and backfill the affected segment.

facts.affected_segment: Florida purchase closings for FNF National Title
facts.remediation: Switch the transform to closing_party_role_id, quarantine normalized_tax_id joins in reconciliation workloads, and backfill Florida purchase closings for FNF National Title
facts.root_cause: settlement_disbursement_recon_v3 joined party_identity_bridge by normalized_tax_id instead of closing_party_role_id, matching multiple buyer and seller party roles within the same closing and duplicating escrow disbursements
